tasks.label: func: host start
args: if command -v func >/dev/null 2>&1; then func host start; else echo "[func: host start] Azure Functions Core Tools (func) not found on PATH; skipping local host startup."; echo "Job host started (skipped: func missing)"; fi

tasks.label: storage: emulator start
args: cd "${workspaceFolder}" && if lsof -iTCP:10000 -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "[storage: emulator start] Azurite already listening on 10000."; exit 0; fi; mkdir -p data_out .azurite; nohup npx --yes azurite --silent --location .azurite --blobPort 10000 --queuePort 10001 --tablePort 10002 > data_out/azurite.log 2>&1 & for i in $(seq 1 30); do if lsof -iTCP:10000 -sTCP:LISTEN >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "[storage: emulator start] Azurite is ready."; exit 0; fi; sleep 1; done; echo "[storage: emulator start] Azurite failed to start."; tail -20 data_out/azurite.log; exit 1

tasks.label: integration:contract-gate-strict-direct-autostart
args: if command -v func >/dev/null 2>&1; then START_FUNC_CMD="func host start" bash ./scripts/integration_contract_gate.sh --strict-endpoints; else echo "[integration:contract-gate-strict-direct-autostart] func not found on PATH; run integration:contract-gate-direct instead."; exit 0; fi

tasks.label: automation: go-forever
args: cd "${workspaceFolder}"; mkdir -p data_out; nohup bash ./scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh >> data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.log 2>&1 & echo started: scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh

tasks.label: automation: go-forever-stop
args: cd "${workspaceFolder}"; if [ -f data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.pid ]; then kill $(cat data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.pid) 2>/dev/null || true; kill -9 $(cat data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.pid) 2>/dev/null || true; fi; for pid in $(pgrep -f "scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh" || true); do [ "$pid" = "$$" ] && continue; kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; done; for pid in $(pgrep -f "repo_health_automation.py --watch --interval 300" || true); do [ "$pid" = "$$" ] && continue; kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; done; echo stopped: scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh

tasks.label: automation: go-forever-restart
args: cd "${workspaceFolder}"; mkdir -p data_out; if [ -f data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.pid ]; then kill $(cat data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.pid) 2>/dev/null || true; fi; for pid in $(pgrep -f "scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh" || true); do [ "$pid" = "$$" ] && continue; kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; done; rm -f data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.pid; rmdir data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.lockdir 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1; nohup bash ./scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh >> data_out/aria_forever_watchdog.log 2>&1 & echo restarted: scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh

tasks.label: automation: repo-health-watch-nonstrict
args: cd "${workspaceFolder}"; mkdir -p data_out/repo_health_automation; pkill -f "repo_health_automation.py --watch --interval 300" 2>/dev/null || true; nohup /workspaces/Aria/.venv/bin/python scripts/repo_health_automation.py --watch --interval 300 --continue-on-fail >> data_out/repo_health_automation/automation.log 2>&1 & echo started: repo_health_automation non-strict

tasks.label: automation: go-forever-status
args: cd "${workspaceFolder}" && if [ -f data_out/aria_forever_watchdog_heartbeat.json ]; then cat data_out/aria_forever_watchdog_heartbeat.json; else echo '{"running":false,"reason":"heartbeat file missing"}'; fi && echo && echo '[watchdog_processes]' && ps aux | grep 'scripts/aria_forever_watchdog.sh' | grep -v grep || true && echo && echo '[repo_health_watchers]' && ps aux | grep 'repo_health_automation.py --watch --interval 300' | grep -v grep || true

tasks.label: smoke: ai/agi/analyze
args: curl -fsS -X POST http://127.0.0.1:7071/api/agi/analyze -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"query\":\"reason through this architecture\"}" | python3 -m json.tool

tasks.label: smoke: ai/agi/stream
args: curl -fsS -N -X POST http://127.0.0.1:7071/api/agi/stream -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"query\":\"stream a short response\"}" | grep -q "data: \\[DONE\\]" && echo "{\"status\":\"ok\",\"done\":true}" | python3 -m json.tool

tasks.label: service: agi install
tasks.detail: Install/refresh the systemd --user service for the AGI launcher and enable it.
args: mkdir -p "$HOME/.config/systemd/user" && cp "${workspaceFolder}/config/aria-agi-launcher.service" "$HOME/.config/systemd/user/aria-agi-launcher.service" && { [ -S "${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/nonexistent}/systemd/private" ] || { echo 'systemd user manager is not available here (e.g. inside this dev container). Unit copied to ~/.config/systemd/user/ — install/enable it on a host with systemd, or use the "startup: agi" task instead.'; exit 1; }; } && systemctl --user daemon-reload && systemctl --user enable aria-agi-launcher.service && echo 'Installed and enabled aria-agi-launcher.service (start it with the "service: agi start" task).'

tasks.label: service: agi start
tasks.detail: Start the AGI launcher user service.
args: { [ -S "${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/nonexistent}/systemd/private" ] || { echo 'systemd user manager is not available here. Use the "startup: agi" task instead, or run on a host with systemd.'; exit 1; }; } && systemctl --user start aria-agi-launcher.service && systemctl --user --no-pager status aria-agi-launcher.service

tasks.label: service: agi stop
tasks.detail: Stop the AGI launcher user service.

tasks.label: service: agi status
tasks.detail: Show status and recent logs for the AGI launcher user service.
args: systemctl --user --no-pager status aria-agi-launcher.service; echo '--- recent logs ---'; journalctl --user -u aria-agi-launcher.service -n 30 --no-pager 2>/dev/null || tail -n 30 "${workspaceFolder}/data_out/aria-agi-launcher.service.log" 2>/dev/null || true

tasks.label: Validate writer-reviewer debug prerequisites
args: for p in 5679 8088; do if lsof -iTCP:$p -sTCP:LISTEN -t >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "[prereq] Port $p already in use"; exit 1; fi; done; echo "[prereq] OK: required debug ports are available (5679, 8088)."

tasks.label: Validate email-spam debug prerequisites
args: for p in 5680 8089; do if lsof -iTCP:$p -sTCP:LISTEN -t >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "[prereq] Port $p already in use"; exit 1; fi; done; echo "[prereq] OK: required debug ports are available (5680, 8089)."

inputs.description: startup: agi — number of launcher cycles (0 = infinite)
startup: agi — seconds to sleep between normal cycles
startup: agi — seconds to sleep after every long-break interval
startup: agi — use the long break after every N cycles
Keep Working — task name to log
Keep Working — work duration in seconds
